                                                                              ZB23.4
STAFF REPORT


To:               Board of Management

From:             Robin D. Hale
                  Chief Operating Officer

Subject:          AFRICAN RAINFOREST PAVILION GENERATOR INSTALLATION

Date:             2018-09-13


SUMMARY:

This report recommends the award of the Quotation for the Generator Installation at
African Rainforest Pavilion, RFQ 27 (2018-07).

RECOMMENDATIONS:

It is recommended that the quotation for Generator Installation – African Rainforest
Pavilion (RFQ 27, 2018-07) be awarded to Tri-Unite Systems in the amount of
$410,563.00, including applicable taxes.

FINANCIAL IMPACT:

Funds are budgeted in the approved 2018 Building and Services Refurbishment Project
for this work.

DECISION HISTORY:

The existing emergency backup generator at the African Rainforest Pavilion is original
and has reached its end-of-life, and was recommended to be replaced in the 2015
Building Audit.

There are many critical life-support systems in the African Rainforest Pavilion which
depend on backup power including the River Fish and Lake Malawi Cichlids Exhibits.
These exhibits require constant power, which means a reliable source of backup power
is vital.

The Toronto Zoo hired Virtual Engineers to provide consulting services for the
replacement generator in 2017. The generator was sized, and the installation details
were designed, and a replacement generator was purchased at the end of 2017, and an
RFQ for installation of the generator was issued in August of 2018.



African Rainforest Generator Installation                                      Page 1 of
3
                                                                            Page 1 of 3
COMMENTS:

The RFQ for installation of the generator at African Rainforest Pavilion was sent to six
contractors, and was posted on the Toronto Zoo website. Four contractors attended the
site inspection meeting held on 2018-07-31, and four bids were received. Of the bids
received, Tri-Unite Systems was the lowest bidder with a bid of $410,563.00 including
applicable taxes. The other three bids were received from Ontario Electrical Construction
Company Ltd., Ferguson Electric Company Ltd., and Thirau Inc., all including applicable
taxes (see Appendix I).




R. D. Hale
Chief Operating Officer


ATTACHMENTS:

Appendix I – Schedule of Tenders Received

                                              (incl. applicable taxes)

   Ainsworth                                           N/A               No Bid

   Black & McDonald                                    N/A               No Bid

   Everbrite Industries Ltd.                           N/A               No Bid

   Ferguson Electric Company Ltd.                  $787,865.20           Meets Requirements

   Tri Unite Systems                               $410,563.00           Meets Requirements

   Ontario Electrical Construction Co. Inc.        $690,109.63           Meets Requirements

   Thirau Inc.                                    $1,044,161.49          Meets Requirements
